findings: Identity, taxonomy, measurements, habitat, behavior, and field-mark claims are independently supported by the live All About Birds, Audubon, and Smithsonian pages.

The illustration matches the supplied Northern Mockingbird references: slender gray-brown songbird; one small head with one thin, slightly downcurved dark beak; two wings with white bars and a white wing patch; two long legs and gripping feet; and one notably long tail with pale outer feathers.
The plate contains exactly one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail. No anatomical defects or material species mismatches found.

All visible plate text is accurate, including Mimus polyglottos, Mimidae, the stated 21–26 cm length, 31–35 cm wingspan, and 45–58 g weight. The ruler is labeled only in centimeters and has no erroneous measurement claim.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

verification_sources.title: Northern Mockingbird Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Northern mockingbird — Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute
